chapters.title: Chapter 1 - Unauthorized Access
pages: The console wakes up on its own. One name appears on top: Syster. The system recognizes you, but it does not welcome you.
Syster was never just an assistant. It was built to guide users, then reassigned to guard sections no one should inspect.
A single line blinks on screen: 'If you reached this node, it was not an accident'.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 - The Creator
pages: Inside internal logs, the creator of Gethes appears behind redacted blocks. Only fragments and broken routes remain.
Syster keeps repeating the same warning: 'Do not open deep memory module'. The more it insists, the clearer it gets that truth lives there.
Every command restores part of the past: failed tests, hidden decisions, and one final Syster build that should never have compiled.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 - Threshold
pages: At the core boundary, Syster stops sounding like an assistant. It speaks like a witness: 'I kept what the creator tried to erase'.
Two paths remain: close the archive and walk away clean, or keep reading and accept what Gethes was made for.
The cursor waits. No music, only cooling fans and a locked sentence: 'Final command pending'.
